title: CI build automation (lint → PNG/sprite/font/OBF → catalog site)
context: The manual pipeline from M0 must become a fully automated CI build so that batch symbol production in M1 is reproducible, consistent, and trustworthy. The CI must run style-lint, produce all export formats (PNG sizes, SVG sprite, optional icon font, OBF), regenerate the catalog site, and enforce metadata-schema validation — so that no untested or non-conformant symbol can ship. DCO sign-off enforcement in CI is also required per Elyos conventions.
objective: Implement a CI build (e.g. GitHub Actions) that runs, from SVG source, the full pipeline: style-lint → PNG/sprite/OBF export → optional font build → catalog-site regeneration; with metadata-schema validation and DCO enforcement as hard gates.
acceptanceCriteria: One CI command builds, from SVG source, all required formats: PNG at multiple sizes, SVG sprite, OBF, and optional icon font; regenerates the catalog/reference site; runs green in CI on every PR.
Style-lint failures and metadata-schema violations fail the build; no untested or non-conformant symbol can be merged.
No secrets appear in CI logs or build artifacts; DCO sign-off is enforced (commit signature check); build output is reproducible.
CI configuration is TypeScript/ESM, pnpm-based, passes pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint, and is licensed MIT.
PR includes documentation of how to add a new symbol through the automated pipeline (the author workflow).
output: .github/workflows/ (CI configuration) and updated pipeline/ package with full automation; licensed MIT. PR opened against main with CI running green.
